title: Use not(slot) as a deterministic overflow storage location that never collides with keccak256 slots
trigger_patterns: secondary storage slot for same key
description: keccak256 outputs are distributed in [0, 2^256) but practically never reach the top of the range. not(slot) = 2^256 - 1 - slot maps any keccak256 slot to the complementary address space, which is provably collision-free with any other keccak256-derived slot. Used by solady as a zero-cost secondary slot for overflow values.
pattern_before:
// Separate mapping for overflow — costs extra keccak256
mapping(bytes32 => uint256) overflowValues;
when_to_apply: When a slot needs a paired secondary location for overflow or extended data
when_not_to_apply: When primary slot is not keccak256-derived (could theoretically collide with low slot numbers)
